title: homalium axillare
intro: Homalium axillare, also known as the Black Olive or Black Elder, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Africa and Asia. It has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including treating fever, digestive issues, and respiratory problems, as well as being used as a natural remedy for wounds and skin conditions. Traditionally, Homalium axillare is prepared as a decoction or infusion for medicinal purposes, and it has also been used in traditional herbal preparations to treat various ailments.
